content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Sherwood H. Patterson 
KGDP Radio station 
P.O. Box 660 
Santa Ynez, CA 
93460 
Dear Mr. Patterson: 
May 7, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-86-136 
This will confirm!! our telephone conversation of April 23, 
1986. 
In that conversation, as well as in your letter of 
April 23, you asked if campaign contributions to a candidate 
for supervisor could result in a conflict of interest under the 
Political Reform Act.~ You indicated that the candidate you 
support for supervisor is currently a city councilmember and 
may be voting in that capacity on a project owned by you. 
section 84308(c) provides that no officer of any agency 
shall make, participate in making, or in any way attempt to use 
his or her official position to influence the decision in a 
proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement 
for use pending before the agency if the officer has willfully 
or knowingly received a contribution in an amount of two 
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more within the preceding 12 
months from a party or his or her agent, or from any 
participant or his or agent. 
However, the term "agency" 
excludes local government agencies whose members are directly 
elected by the voters. 
section 84308(a) (3). 
Therefore, 
Section 84308 does not apply to members of the city councilor 
to the county board of supervisors when acting in those 
respective capacities. 
!I This confirmation is being responded to by informal 
assistance rather than written advice (see Commission 
regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code section 18329, enclosed). 
~ Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


Sherwood H. Patterson 
May 7, 1986 
Page 2 
section 84308 does apply however, when a city councilmember 
or county supervisor sits as an appointed member of another 
board or commission. 
The enclosed pamphlet discusses how 
Section 84308 applies and provides examples of how elected 
officials sitting on appointed bodies are affected. 
After reviewing the pamphlet if you have any additional 
questions, please feel free to call me at (916) 322-9501. 
JGM:JET:plh 
Enclosure 
X2>~reJ4. ~ ~ 
~. 
McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division 


April 23, 1986 
Mr. John Mclean 
Staff Counsel 
ORCUTT/SANTA MARIA 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 "J" Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Potential Conflict of Interest During Political Campaign 
Dear Mr. McLean: 
Based on our telephone conversation of April 23, 1986, it is my understanding 
that no conflict of interest is created from contributions to a political cam-
paign. 
These contributions may take the form of cash or the contribution of 
one's time and efforts. 
Specifically, in our instance, you advised me that it was acceptable for us 
to participate in the campaign of a local candidate for County Supervisor when 
that candidate is currently a City Councilman facing a vote in the future on 
a project which we own. 
If you have any difference in understanding our conversation, please contact 
me immediately. 
Also, please forward any guidelines or printed information 
that will clarify this question. 
Si ncerely, 
KGDP RADIO STATION 
(J~1-~~~ 
Sherwood H. Patterson 
SHP:dd 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Sherwood H. Patterson 
KGDP Radio Station 
P.O. Box 660 
Santa Ynez, CA 
93460 
Dear Mr. Patterson: 
April 28, 1986 
Re: 
86-136 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days.
